goal: Persistent TH poster tagline overflow: make wrapping bulletproof + actually deliver it past the edge cache
done: ROBUSTNESS: tokenizeText now ALWAYS character/cluster-flows Thai/Lao/CJK/Khmer (no everyWordFits heuristic that could mis-measure and clip); new toBreakUnits keeps Thai base+vowel+tone-mark clusters intact; fitDailyQuoteText acceptance also requires every line <= maxWidth at the draw font. DELIVERY: the app loaded poster.js via share.js static import behind a 4h /share/* edge cache (unhashed) so fixes never reached users. Now share/index.html (served fresh) loads share.js?v=20260620g; share.js reads that version from import.meta.url and dynamic-imports ./poster.js?v=<same>. Bumping the one ?v= in index.html delivers fresh share.js+poster.js with no manual purge.
root_cause: (1) word-fit heuristic could pass yet render wider -> clip; (2) Cloudflare edge cache (4h, _headers override) served stale poster.js to users.
fix: always-char-flow Thai + per-line width clamp; version-threaded cache-bust from the fresh share HTML through share.js into the poster.js import.
verified: direct buildPoster render Eight-of-Wands -> 3 centred lines offset +4px
full share flow: requests share.js?v= -> poster.js?v=, poster 1080x1920 generated
notes: Convention: bump the ?v= in share/index.html on any share.js/poster.js change. Other /js/* still need the CF dashboard Browser-Cache-TTL set to Respect-Existing-Headers + one-time purge.
